channel.title: OT/ICS Threat Intel - Marine
channel.description: Curated OT/ICS advisories, incidents and threat intelligence. Editorial by Mike Holcomb in partnership with UtilSec.

item.title: [Critical] SonicWall SMA 1000 zero-days under active attack — and a reportable incident under the USCG cyber rule if one is yours
item.description: SonicWall confirmed active exploitation of two Secure Mobile Access 1000 flaws after investigating multiple cases. CVE-2026-15409 is a CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated server-side request forgery in the Work Place interface; CVE-2026-15410 (CVSS 7.2) lets an authenticated administrator inject OS commands through the Appliance Management Console. CISA added both to the KEV catalog on 14 July with a 17 July federal deadline. For ports, terminals and vessel operators the placement is what matters: this class of appliance brokers the shoreside and vendor access that reaches terminal operating systems, crane and gate control, and satcom-backhauled vessel networks. There is a regulatory edge too — under the USCG Marine Transportation System rule, reportable cyber incidents must go to the National Response Center immediately on discovery, so a confirmed compromise here is a notification decision, not just a patch. - SonicWall PSIRT (SNWLID-2026-0008) / CISA KEV / The Hacker News - SonicWall advisory & KEV addition 14 Jul 2026 — reporting 15 Jul; exploitation ongoing

item.title: [High] Lantronix serial-to-IP converter flaw exploited in the wild — the bridges between shipboard serial gear and the network
item.description: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2025-67038 in Lantronix serial-to-IP converters, one of the flaws disclosed in April through the BRIDGE:BREAK research project. This category deserves particular attention at sea and on the quayside. Maritime OT is still overwhelmingly serial at the sensor and actuator layer — NMEA feeds, engine and auxiliary monitoring, alarm and monitoring panels, cargo and ballast systems, crane and gate equipment — and serial-to-IP converters are what connected all of it to shipboard or terminal Ethernet during retrofits and digitalisation projects. They are typically installed by an integrator, absent from any drawing, and never patched because the equipment behind them still works. Exploitation is confirmed and follows an explicit OT threat warning; for most operators the first task is establishing what is actually installed. - CVE-2025-67038 - Disclosed Apr 2026 — exploitation reported 25 Jun 2026; ongoing

item.title: [Critical] July ICS Patch Tuesday: Siemens ships a CVSS 10 auth bypass fix; Rockwell patches critical controller DoS flaws
item.description: The July 2026 cycle matters to terminals and shipyards running mainstream industrial automation. Siemens published nine advisories, six critical — headlined by a CVSS 10 token-invalidation flaw in Opencenter X allowing authentication bypass and full application access, with further critical fixes in Simatic S7-1500, Mendix, Sidis Secured SmartPlug, Cadra and Desigo CC (the last being building-management software common in terminal and port facilities). Rockwell published 12 advisories including three critical denial-of-service holes in CompactLogix, ControlLogix, Compact GuardLogix and GuardLogix controllers that can force a major non-recoverable fault, plus a critical unauthenticated CLI exposure in 1715 Redundant I/O. Schneider fixed a high-severity code-execution flaw in IGSS SCADA. VDE CERT added five advisories covering Codesys and Wago among others. - Siemens ProductCERT / Schneider / Rockwell PSIRT / SecurityWeek - Published 14 Jul 2026 (Patch Tuesday) — reporting 15 Jul

item.title: [High] BeyondTrust patches critical auth-bypass flaws in the privileged remote access tooling vendors use
item.description: BeyondTrust disclosed two authentication-bypass vulnerabilities on 7 July in Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA). CVE-2026-40138 (CVSS 9.2) stems from improper validation of authentication data and could let a network-positioned attacker bypass access controls and reach the appliance, including elevated accounts; CVE-2026-40139 involves improper processing of RS authentication requests. Both require a specific authentication configuration to be enabled, which limits exposure, and neither is under active exploitation — no public proof-of-concept exists. Cloud customers were patched on 21 April; self-hosted RS/PRA at 25.3.2 or earlier needs action. Alongside the SonicWall top story, the pattern is clear enough: vendor remote maintenance — the standing route OEMs use into vessel and terminal systems — is under sustained research attention. - CVE-2026-40138 - Disclosed 7 Jul 2026

item.title: [High] CISA advisories cover Digi PortServer TS and Digi One SP IA9 serial device servers
item.description: CISA released seven ICS advisories on 7 July, among them Digi International PortServer TS and Digi One SP IA9 — serial device servers from the same family as the Lantronix converters currently being exploited (see Incidents). The rest of the batch covers Hitachi Energy PROMOD V and e-mesh EMS, Siemens Mendix Studio Pro and SINEC OS, Labcenter Proteus 9 and the Hydro-Québec Le Circuit Électrique charging-station backend; those are energy- and water-flagged rather than maritime. The Digi entries are the maritime-relevant part, and worth reading together with the Lantronix exploitation: two advisories against the humble serial-server category inside a fortnight suggests researchers are looking hard at it, which has historically preceded attackers doing the same. - CISA ICS advisories / WaterISAC - CISA advisories published 7 Jul 2026

item.title: [High] CISA’s 14 July batch carries three ABB advisories — worth a look given ABB’s marine automation footprint
item.description: CISA issued four ICS advisories on 14 July (ICSA-26-195-01 through -04), released alongside vendor Patch Tuesday advisories: three from ABB and one from Rockwell. The ABB entries cover Advant Master Online Builder, where an incorrect Online Builder version was included on installation media, and Ability Edgenius, where a Linux kernel privilege-escalation issue could let a locally authenticated user or a compromised container workload gain root. To be precise about scope: these are ABB industrial products, not ABB Marine systems, so they do not name shipboard equipment. They are flagged here because ABB is a significant supplier into marine propulsion, power and automation, and operators with mixed ABB estates ashore — at terminals, yards and support facilities — should check the affected version lists rather than assume the advisories miss them. - ICSA-26-195-01 … -04 - CISA advisories published 14 Jul 2026

item.title: [Info] The USCG cyber rule’s big deadline is 16 July 2027: Cybersecurity Officer, assessment and plan
item.description: A forward-looking compliance marker rather than news. Under the Coast Guard’s Cybersecurity in the Marine Transportation System final rule, U.S.-flagged vessels, OCS facilities and waterfront facilities must, by 16 July 2027, designate a Cybersecurity Officer, complete an initial cybersecurity assessment (annually thereafter) and submit a Cybersecurity Plan to the USCG for approval. Two earlier milestones are already live and worth confirming you meet: reportable cyber incidents have had to go to the National Response Center immediately on discovery since 16 July 2025, and the personnel cybersecurity training requirement took effect 16 January 2026. The Coast Guard has also signalled that Port State Control scrutiny will extend to indicators of poor cybersecurity practice on foreign-flagged vessels, with deficiencies, detention or denial of entry available as outcomes. - US Coast Guard / Maritime Commons - Final rule effective 16 Jul 2025; training due 16 Jan 2026; CySO, assessment & plan due 16 Jul 2027
